dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses standard Pivot Point calculations (P, R1, S1, R2, S2) which are available in the allowed indicators. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT based on price positioning relative to pivot support/resistance levels. Entry conditions trigger when price is between S1-P (bullish zone) or between S2-S1 (bearish zone). Exit conditions use crossover operators for when price penetrates key levels. Lookback is 1 day (prior session data) which uses prev_high, prev_low, prev_close implicitly through pivot calculations.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses standard Pivot Point calculations (P, R1, S1, R2, S2) which are available in the allowed indicators. Direction is CONTEXT_DEPENDENT based on price positioning relative to pivot support/resistance levels. Entry conditions trigger when price is between S1-P (bullish zone) or between S2-S1 (bearish zone). Exit conditions use crossover operators for when price penetrates key levels. Lookback is 1 day (prior session data) which uses prev_high, prev_low, prev_close implicitly through pivot calculations.

source_rule_text: Calculate pivot point and support/resistance levels using prior day's high, low, and close; then project next day's support and resistance levels based on these calculations.
